text:
There once was a fox named Percival who lived high up in an oak tree. He was well-fed, charming, and could talk intelligently about owls. Every winter, he'd hear the lovely voices of sirens lamenting about lost love. He fancied themselves quite beautiful.

One day, Percival decided he wanted to meet these sirens. So he crafted a tiny cage out of branches and feathers. They didn't seem fazed by his presence!

Each night, the sirens would sing a haunting melody. Percival listened patiently. One evening, the leader sighed deeply.

"I haven't caught anything since winter turned into spring."

"That's quite a burden," Percival said softly. "Why don't you go listen at an old river?"

A year passed. The sirens grew stronger. They returned yearly.

Percival grew old. They honored him. He grew quiet. One last night...

"I've sung a year," he said. "I think I've found
